# Overview

![][image1]

# Home Page

**STARTUP GARAGE**

Website Copy — Homepage

*Version 1.0  |  For review & editing*

**COPY NOTE:** *All copy is written in Startup Garage's voice — warm, faith-aware, practical, never corporate. Orange labels indicate copy notes or design directions for the web team.*

**SECTION 1 — HERO**

**COPY NOTE:** *Full-width section, black background, white text, orange accent. Single strong headline. One CTA button. Consider a photo or short video loop of a real founder or Forge group in the background.*

**Built for Founders Who Build for More**

Startup Garage is a nonprofit equipping early-stage, faith-driven entrepreneurs to launch real ventures through events, intentional connections, and a small group community called The Forge.

It's completely free. And it starts with showing up.

**→ Find an Event** 

**→ Learn About The Forge**

**COPY NOTE:** *Two CTAs is intentional — one for the 'I'm just curious' visitor, one for the 'I'm ready' visitor. Both should be visible in the hero without scrolling on desktop.*

**SECTION 2 — THE PATHWAY**

**COPY NOTE:** *White or light gray background. Three-column card layout with a visible progression arrow between each step. Fellowship card should have an orange border to signal it's the destination. The Forge gets its own callout box below the three columns.*

**One Pathway. Your Pace.**

We meet founders where they are. Whether you're still figuring out your idea or ready to go all in there's a place for you here.

**Events — Explore & Learn**

Open to everyone. No application, no commitment. Just come.

We host workshops, speaker nights, and pitch competitions throughout the year and we partner with local universities to bring these experiences directly to students and campus programs as well as the local community.

* Workshops on business, funding, and faith in work  
* Guest speakers from across industries  
* Pitch competitions and startup showcases  
* University and campus program partnerships

**→ See Upcoming Events**

**Connections — Clarity & Next Steps**

When you're ready for more than a conversation in the hallway, Connections brings you face-to-face with experienced coaches who can help you think through your next move.

Our coaches bring expertise across industries and business stages. We match you based on where you are and what you need, not a public directory or a self-serve booking link. Every connection is intentional.

* 1:1 coaching conversations  
* Matched by expertise and your specific needs  
* Access to tools and resources to keep you moving

**→ Request a Connection**

**Groups — Build & Grow**

The deepest level of Startup Garage. “The Forge” is where founders stop going it alone.

Through mentorship from deeply vetted leaders and access to our small groups called “The Forge,” you'll find the community that helps you build your venture and live out your calling.

* Mentorship from leaders who've been where you are  
* Participation in The Forge, small groups for founders  
* Special events and curated founder experiences

**→ Learn About The Forge**

**SECTION 3 — THE FORGE CALLOUT**

**COPY NOTE:** *This should feel distinct from the three pathway columns — use a black or dark background block with orange accents. It's the destination, and the design should signal that. Consider a pull quote from a current Forge participant here.*

**The Forge**

Small groups doing life together with support for faith, family, and business as founders build their ventures.

The Forge isn't a class or a time-bound cohort. It's a small group of founders who meet regularly, go deep, and walk through the hard parts of building something together. Faith-centered. Relationship-first. Practically grounded.

Spots are limited. Participation is free.

**→ Apply for The Forge**

**SECTION 4 — WHO WE SERVE**

**COPY NOTE:** *Two-column or four-card grid layout. Clean, minimal. Black arrows or orange accent marks before each item.*

**You Belong Here If...**

You have an idea you can't stop thinking about.

You're early-stage and building something from scratch.

You want to build a business that reflects your faith and values.

You're a student, a young professional, or a local community member ready to take the next step.

You're not sure what you are yet, but you want to be around people who are figuring it out too.

**COPY NOTE:** *Consider a subtle line below this: 'Startup Garage is free for all participants. Always.' — this is a major differentiator and should appear early and often.*

**SECTION 5 — FOUNDER STORIES / TESTIMONIALS**

**COPY NOTE:** *Use the full-page feature section layout (Option 3\) — large photo, bold pull quote, minimal supporting text. Rotate between 2–3 founder stories. This section should feel human and editorial, not like a standard testimonials block.*

**What Founders Are Saying**

**\[Founder Name\], \[Venture Name\]**

*"\[Pull quote — 1–2 sentences from a real founder. Should speak to community, direction, or what Startup Garage made possible.\]"*

*PLACEHOLDER — replace with real quote when available*

**\[Founder Name\], \[Venture Name\]**

*"\[Pull quote — focus on mentorship, The Forge, or a turning point moment.\]"*

*PLACEHOLDER — replace with real quote when available*

**SECTION 6 — FOOTER CTA BANNER**

**COPY NOTE:** *Black background, white text, orange CTA button. Should mirror the energy of the hero. This is the last thing someone sees before they leave — make it count.*

**Start with an Event. Take Your Next Step. Find Intentional Community in The Forge.**

Startup Garage is open to everyone. Free for all participants. Rooted in West Michigan and growing.

**→ Find an Event**

**→ Get Involved**

**NAVIGATION & META COPY**

**COPY NOTE:** *For the web team — suggested nav labels, tagline, and meta description.*

**Primary Navigation (5 tabs)**

* Our Pathway  
* Events  
* Coaches & Mentors  
* The Forge  
* Resources & Tools

**Site Tagline** 

* Expanding Christ-Centered Entrepreneurship

**Meta Description (for SEO)**

Startup Garage is a nonprofit equipping early-stage, faith-driven founders through free events, coaching connections, mentorship, and small groups called The Forge.

*Startup Garage Website Copy v1.0  |  Prepared for internal review*

# Coaches & Mentors

**STARTUP GARAGE**

**Website Copy — Coaches & Mentors**

*Version 1.0  |  For review & editing*

**COPY NOTE:** *All copy is written in Startup Garage’s voice — warm, faith-aware, practical, never corporate. Orange labels indicate copy notes or design directions for the web team.*

**SECTION 1 — HERO**

**COPY NOTE:** *Full-width section, black background, white text, orange accent. Match the homepage hero treatment (same typography scale, same CTA button style). Two CTAs visible without scrolling on desktop — one for the “just exploring” visitor, one for the “ready to connect” visitor.*

**The Right Voice at the Right Moment**

Every founder hits a wall. A legal question you can’t Google your way out of. A pitch deck that still isn’t landing. A hiring decision that feels bigger than it should. You don’t have to figure it out alone.

Startup Garage connects you with experienced coaches and vetted mentors who’ve walked this road and want to walk it with you.

Free for all participants. Personally matched. No public booking links, no cold outreach — just the right conversation at the right time.

**→ Request a Connection**     *\[LINK TO: Intake Form\]*

**→ See How It Works**     *Anchor scroll to “How It Works” section below*

**COPY NOTE:** *Two CTAs is intentional — primary is the intake form for ready-to-go founders; secondary lets skeptical visitors understand the process before committing.*

**SECTION 2 — COACHES VS. MENTORS**

**COPY NOTE:** *Two-column side-by-side layout, equal column width. Use icons if available (no headshots in this section — coaches are never shown individually, and mentors are featured further down the page). Clean dividing rule or spacing between the two columns.*

**Two Words, Used on Purpose**

Coaches and mentors play different roles in a founder’s journey. Knowing the difference helps you ask for what you actually need and helps us match you well.

| COACHES Targeted help, when you need it What they are: Industry experts who step in for a specific question, project, or short season. When to ask for one: You need focused help. A go to market strategy. A pricing gut-check. A pitch deck critique. A marketing plan you can actually follow. How it works: You tell us what you’re working on. We match you to someone in the right area. These coaches offer a 30-minute virtual coaching meeting to guide you with your specific needs. | MENTORS Ongoing relationship, built over time What they are: Deeply vetted leaders who walk with founders over the long haul. Higher bar to become one. Usually seasoned entrepreneurs, operators, or investors themselves. When to ask for one: You’re looking for an intentional, ongoing relationship with someone who learns your business, challenges your thinking, and shows up in the harder seasons. How it works: Mentorship is a thoughtful match and is embedded within our small groups called “The Forge”. To join a Forge group and be paired with a mentor, apply here. |
| :---- | :---- |

**SECTION 3 — HOW IT WORKS**

**COPY NOTE:** *Three-step horizontal progression. Numbered 1 / 2 / 3 in the same visual style as “The Pathway” section on the homepage (three columns on desktop, stacked on mobile). Keep copy tight. Orange accent on the numbers.*

**One Path In**

No awkward cold DMs. No public directories to scroll through. No self-booking. You tell us what you need, and we do the matching so you can focus on the work.

**1\.  Tell us what you’re working on**

Fill out the information in our Startup Garage portal. It takes about five minutes. The more specific you are about what you need, the better the match will be.

**2\.  We make the match**

A Startup Garage team member reviews your request and connects you with the right coach or mentor from our network. Most founders hear back within a week.

**3\.  You meet — free**

Every conversation is free for participants. Always. No surprise fees, no upsells, no hidden program costs. Just real help from someone who’s done it before.

**→ Start Your Intake**     *\[LINK TO: Create a profile on the Startup Garage portal\]*

**SECTION 4 — COACH CATEGORIES**

**COPY NOTE:** *Eight-card grid (4x2 on desktop, 2x4 on tablet, stacked on mobile). Each card shows the category title and a short description. NO individual coach names. NO booking links. NO headshots. Cards are visual only — they do not link out.*

**Where Our Coaches Can Help**

If your question doesn’t neatly fit one of these categories, ask anyway. We’ll figure it out together.

**Marketing & Branding**

Positioning, messaging, storytelling, content, social, email, and ads that actually convert.

**Legal**

Entity formation, contracts, IP basics, partnership agreements, and common employment questions.

**Finance & Accounting**

Bookkeeping, cash flow, pricing, financial modeling, and tax-adjacent questions (for the complicated stuff, we’ll refer you to a pro).

**Sales**

Outbound strategy, pipeline design, discovery conversations, closing, and building a repeatable process.

**Operations**

Systems, tools, hiring your first teammates, and standing up processes that can actually scale.

**Product & Tech**

MVP decisions, tech stack choices, finding developers, user research, and the path to product-market fit.

**HR & People**

Hiring, onboarding, team culture, handling tough conversations, and leading well as you grow.

**Fundraising**

Readiness check, pitch critique, investor lists, understanding terms, and knowing when not to raise.

**SECTION 5 — FEATURED MENTORS**

**COPY NOTE:** *Full-width feature blocks, styled like the Founder Stories layout on the homepage — large photo on one side, bold name \+ focus area \+ short bio on the other. Feature 3–4 mentors total. Alternate photo-left / photo-right for visual rhythm.*

**Meet a Few of Our Forge Mentors**

Our mentors are some of the most generous, experienced leaders in West Michigan and beyond. They’re here because they’ve built things, lost things, learned plenty, and want to help the next generation of founders do it a little better.

This is a small highlight of our mentor bench — not a full directory. Connections are made through the intake process, not through this page. These mentors lead our small groups called “The Forge”

**Featured Mentor 1**

*\[MENTOR NAME\], \[FOCUS AREA\]*  
*“\[Two-to-three sentence bio — professional background plus a personal note about what they care about, who they love to help, or why they say yes to this work.\]”*

*PLACEHOLDER — replace with real mentor details and headshot before launch*

**Featured Mentor 2**

*\[MENTOR NAME\], \[FOCUS AREA\]*  
*“\[Two-to-three sentence bio — professional background plus a personal note about what they care about, who they love to help, or why they say yes to this work.\]”*

*PLACEHOLDER — replace with real mentor details and headshot before launch*

**Featured Mentor 3**

*\[MENTOR NAME\], \[FOCUS AREA\]*  
*“\[Two-to-three sentence bio — professional background plus a personal note about what they care about, who they love to help, or why they say yes to this work.\]”*

*PLACEHOLDER — replace with real mentor details and headshot before launch*

**COPY NOTE:** *Optional fourth featured mentor if layout allows without crowding. Do not publish with placeholder content visible.*

**SECTION 6 — FOOTER CTA BANNER**

**COPY NOTE:** *Full-width banner, dark or orange background (mirror the energy of the homepage footer CTA banner). Single primary CTA. Reinforcement line below in a smaller size.*

**One Conversation Can Change Your Week**

Whatever you’re building, there’s probably someone in our network who’s wrestled with exactly what’s on your plate today. Tell us what you need. We’ll take it from there.

Free for all participants. Always.

**→ Request a Connection**     *\[LINK TO: Intake Form\]*

*Startup Garage Website Copy v1.0  |  Prepared for internal review*

# The Forge

**STARTUP GARAGE**

**Website Copy — The Forge**

*Version 1.0  |  For review & editing*

**COPY NOTE:** *All copy is written in Startup Garage’s voice — warm, faith-aware, practical, never corporate. The Forge is positioned as a community destination, not a program or cohort. Christ-centered framing appears more explicitly here than on other pages. Orange labels indicate copy notes or design directions for the web team.*

**SECTION 1 — HERO**

**COPY NOTE:** *Black or deep-charcoal background, white text, orange accent. Match the intimate, weighty energy of the homepage Forge callout block (not the lighter hero treatment used on Coaches & Mentors). Single primary CTA. If available, use a photo of a real Forge group meeting — people, not product shots.*

**You Were Never Meant to Build Alone**

The Forge is a small group of founders who meet regularly, go deep, and walk through the hard parts of building something together. Faith-centered. Relationship-first. Practically grounded.

Spots are limited. Participation is always free.

**→ Apply for The Forge**     *\[LINK TO: Forge Application\]*

**SECTION 2 — WHAT THE FORGE IS**

**COPY NOTE:** *Clean white or light background. Use a clear visual distinction between the “What it is” and “What it isn’t” lists (side-by-side columns on desktop, stacked on mobile). The word “Christ-centered” is intentional and should appear as written.*

**What The Forge Is — and Isn’t**

Most founders don’t need another networking group. They need a table. A small, trusted circle where they can be honest about the work, the doubts, and the bigger calling underneath it all.

The Forge is Startup Garage’s answer to the loneliness most founders carry in silence. It’s where the work gets heavier and lighter at the same time.

**What it is**

\*  A small group of Christ-centered founders doing life together

\*  Built around faith, family, and business — the three things that either hold together or quietly fall apart

\*  A regular rhythm of meeting, praying, problem-solving, and real talk

\*  A place to bring the stuff you don’t say in LinkedIn posts

**What it isn’t**

\*  A class or curriculum

\*  A cohort with a start and end date

\*  A networking group disguised as something deeper

\*  An exclusive club — the bar to join is honesty, not status

**SECTION 3 — WHAT TO EXPECT**

**COPY NOTE:** *Four stacked cards or a two-by-two grid. Keep language relational, not operational. Intentionally general: group size, cadence, and location details stay off the site so we don’t have to update copy every time those shift.*

**What to Expect**

We keep things simple. No fancy curriculum, no guest speakers every week, no forced icebreakers.

**Small, trusted groups**

Groups stay small enough to know each other’s stories. You’ll build real relationships, not collect contacts.

**A steady rhythm**

Forge groups meet regularly on a monthly basis. Consistency is how trust is built. Show up, and the rest takes care of itself.

**Real talk, every time**

We make space for what actually matters: the business wins, the business losses, the family stuff, the faith stuff. Nothing is off the table. And nothing leaves the room.

**Always free**

Every Startup Garage offering is free for participants. The Forge is no exception. No dues, no fees, no hidden costs.

**SECTION 4 — WHO IT’S FOR**

**COPY NOTE:** *Match the layout of the “You Belong Here If…” section on the homepage. Asterisk bullets, short lines. Inclusive and inviting tone — not a checklist of qualifications.*

**The Forge Is for You If…**

\*  You’re building a venture and you’re tired of building it alone.

\*  Your faith matters to you and you want a community that takes it seriously.

\*  You’ve got business questions, family tensions, and a calling you can’t shake.

\*  You’re willing to show up — even when it’s easier not to.

\*  You want honest friendships more than a polished network.

If you read that list and thought “that’s me,” the next step is the application. If you read it and thought “maybe, but…,” apply anyway. We’ll talk.

**SECTION 5 — A FORGE VOICE**

**COPY NOTE:** *Full-page feature section (Option 3 layout) — large photo on one side, bold pull quote on the other. Keep supporting text minimal. Placeholder content below; real Forge participant quote and photo to be supplied before launch.*

**In Their Words**

*“\[One to three sentences from a current or recent Forge participant. Speak to what The Forge made possible — community in a hard season, a faith conversation at the right time, a friendship that outlasted the business decision.\]”*

**\[FORGE PARTICIPANT NAME\], \[VENTURE NAME\]**

*PLACEHOLDER — replace with real quote, attribution, and photo before launch.*

**SECTION 6 — HOW TO APPLY**

**COPY NOTE:** *Plain section, centered or left-aligned. Honest tone about the process — not every applicant is placed immediately. If groups are full, the waitlist is a soft landing, not a dead end.*

**How to Apply**

Applying to The Forge is a short conversation, not a long form. We ask a handful of questions about you, your venture, and where faith fits in. Then we meet — in person when we can, over video when we can’t.

Two reasons we do it this way. First, we want to make sure The Forge is actually the right fit for where you are. Second, we want to find the right group for you — the people you’ll grow with.

**Timing**

Most applicants hear back within two weeks. If groups are full, we’ll place you on our interest list and reach out as soon as a spot opens.

**→ Apply for The Forge**     *\[LINK TO: Forge Application\]*

**SECTION 7 — FOOTER CTA BANNER**

**COPY NOTE:** *Black or dark background, white text, orange CTA button. Match the homepage footer CTA energy. Short reinforcement line below the button.*

**Come Build Alongside Us**

The Forge is small by design, free by commitment, and deeper than most founders expect. If you’re ready for a community that takes your faith, your family, and your venture seriously, we’d love to meet you.

Spots are limited. Participation is free. Always.

**→ Apply for The Forge**     *\[LINK TO: Forge Application\]*

*Startup Garage Website Copy v1.0  |  Prepared for internal review*

# Events

**STARTUP GARAGE**

**Website Copy — Events**

*Version 1.0  |  For review & editing*

**COPY NOTE:** *All copy is written in Startup Garage’s voice — warm, faith-aware, practical, never corporate. Events are the front door of Startup Garage — open to everyone, free, and low-commitment. Orange labels indicate copy notes or design directions for the web team.*

**SECTION 1 — HERO**

**COPY NOTE:** *Match homepage hero scale. Warm, welcoming tone. Two CTAs visible without scrolling — primary sends visitors to the events feed, secondary anchors to “What to Expect” for first-timers who need a little more before committing.*

**Just Come.**

Events are the easiest way to meet Startup Garage. No application. No commitment. No cost. Show up and see what we’re about.

We host workshops, speaker nights, and pitch competitions across West Michigan and partner with local colleges to bring Startup Garage programming directly to students and campus entrepreneurship programs in addition to the local community.

**→ Find an Event**    *\[LINK TO: Events Feed \- Eventbrite\]*

**→ See What to Expect**     *Anchor scroll to “What to Expect” section below*

**SECTION 2 — UPCOMING EVENTS**

**COPY NOTE:** *Placeholder for the live events feed. Web team will wire this to Squarespace Events (or whatever feed source SG uses). Ideal layout: 3–4 event cards showing title, date, location, short description, and RSVP link. If no events are scheduled, a friendly fallback message plus an email sign-up.*

**What’s Coming Up**

Everything we’re putting on next. Every event is free and open to anyone who wants to come.

| *\[LIVE EVENTS FEED — 3–4 cards displayed here\]* *Each card shows: Event Title  ·  Date & Time  ·  Location  ·  Short Description  ·  → RSVP Link* *Fallback state when no events are scheduled:* *“Nothing on the calendar this week — new events go up about a month out. Want us to let you know when the next one drops? Drop your email below.”  \+  email capture field.* |
| :---- |

**→ See All Upcoming Events**     *\[LINK TO: Full Events Calendar \- Eventbrite\]*

**SECTION 3 — EVENT TYPES**

**COPY NOTE:** *Four-card grid (2x2 on desktop, stacked on mobile). Each card gets a short description and a subtle orange accent. These are the four categories visitors will see on the calendar throughout the year.*

**What We Put On**

Different events for different seasons. Here’s what you’ll find on our calendar over the course of a year.

**Workshops**

Practical, hands-on sessions on business, funding, marketing, and faith-in-work questions that don’t get asked at most conferences. Led by practitioners who’ve done the work, not just talked about it.

**Speaker Nights**

Industry leaders and experienced founders sharing real stories — the wins, the losses, and the hard-earned lessons they wish someone had told them earlier. Come hungry for questions.

**Pitch Competitions & Showcases**

Opportunities for early-stage founders to pitch their ventures, get sharp feedback, and compete for microgrants and other resources. Judged by coaches, mentors, and investors from the SG network.

**University & Campus Partnerships**

We plug into campus entrepreneurship programming across West Michigan with guest speakers, workshop facilitators, and pitch competition judges. More on this in the section below.

**SECTION 4 — WHAT TO EXPECT**

**COPY NOTE:** *Short, welcoming, first-timer friendly. Addresses the “should I even come?” question directly. Five asterisk bullets. Keep it practical and disarming.*

**New Here? Here’s What to Expect**

Most people show up to their first Startup Garage event wondering if they’ll fit. They do. Here’s what every event looks like:

\*  You don’t need to apply or pay — just RSVP and show up.

\*  Everyone is welcome: founders, students, curious locals, and the faith-is-interesting-but-I’m-not-sure crowd.

\*  Every event has a clear start and end time, so you can come and go as your schedule allows.

\*  Most events include food or coffee. We try to make showing up feel easy.

\*  You’ll leave with at least one new conversation, one new idea, and probably a business card or two.

Startup Garage is free for all participants. Always.

**SECTION 5 — UNIVERSITY PARTNERSHIPS**

**COPY NOTE:** *Full-width dedicated section with distinct visual treatment from the rest of the page (dark background or large orange accent block). This speaks directly to university entrepreneurship directors, faculty, and student life leads — not to founders. Do NOT reference cohorts or cohort-based programs; that model has been retired.*

**For Universities & Campus Programs**

If you run a student entrepreneurship program, an incubator, a startup course, or a campus pitch competition, we’d love to plug in.

Startup Garage doesn’t run our own programs on your campus. Instead, we add value to the programming you already have by bringing in trusted practitioners who step into your existing calendar.

**Ways We Show Up**

\*  Guest speakers for classes, chapel, or campus events.

\*  Workshop facilitators for entrepreneurship programs or student organizations.

\*  Judges and mentors for pitch competitions and demo days.

\*  Faith-in-work and founder-life conversations for student life programming.

We serve schools across West Michigan and are always open to new relationships. Reach out and tell us what you’re working on and we’ll figure out how we can help.

**→ Partner With Us**     *\[LINK TO: University Partnership Inquiry\]*

**SECTION 6 — FOOTER CTA BANNER**

**COPY NOTE:** *Match the homepage footer CTA energy. Black or dark background, white text, orange CTA button. Short reinforcement line below.*

**Start with Showing Up**

Every founder who finds their people here started the same way. They came to one event. That’s all it takes. The rest unfolds from there.

Free for all participants. Always.

**→ Find an Event**     *\[LINK TO: Events Feed\]*

*Startup Garage Website Copy v1.0  |  Prepared for internal review*

# Our Pathway

**STARTUP GARAGE**

**Website Copy — Our Pathway**

*Version 1.0  |  For review & editing*

**COPY NOTE:** *This page is the deeper expansion of the three-step pathway shown on the homepage. Same three tiers — Events, Connections, Small Groups — but given room to breathe, with founder stories and a “Where to Start” decision helper at the end. Voice is warm, faith-aware, practical, never corporate. Orange labels indicate copy notes or design directions for the web team.*

**SECTION 1 — HERO**

**COPY NOTE:** *Match homepage hero scale. The hero introduces the idea that building something meaningful is a journey — and that Startup Garage walks it with founders in three progressive steps. One primary CTA anchors to the pathway; secondary anchors to “Where to Start.”*

**A Path for Founders Who Want to Build Something That Lasts.**

Building a business is hard. Building one that reflects who you are, what you believe, and what you’re called to is harder still. No one does it alone.

Our pathway is simple. Three steps, each one deeper than the last. You don’t have to take them all at once. You just have to take the next one.

**→ See the Pathway**     *Anchor scroll to “The Pathway” section below*

**→ Help Me Find My Starting Point**     *Anchor scroll to “Where to Start” section below*

**SECTION 2 — WHY SG EXISTS**

**COPY NOTE:** *Light touch — short mission paragraph only. This page is about the founder journey, not about Startup Garage itself. Just enough to ground the reader in why this pathway exists before we walk them through it.*

**Why This Pathway Exists**

Startup Garage exists because faith-driven founders need more than good ideas and capital. They need people who’ve walked the road, rooms where the hard questions belong, and relationships that go the distance.

We built this pathway because most founders don’t need another webinar or cohort. They need a way in, a way forward, and a way deeper. So that’s what we offer.

**SECTION 3 — THE PATHWAY**

**COPY NOTE:** *This is the core of the page. Each of the three tiers gets its own subsection with a numbered label (01, 02, 03), a name, a short descriptor, and a fuller expansion than the homepage offers. Use consistent visual rhythm across all three. Each tier ends with an inline arrow CTA to the relevant page.*

**Three Steps. Your Pace.**

Most founders start at the beginning and move through the pathway as they’re ready. A few jump in further along. Either works. What matters is that wherever you are, there’s a next step.

**01  ·  Events  ·  Explore**

The front door. No application, no cost, no commitment, just show up and see what Startup Garage is about. Workshops, speaker nights, pitch competitions, and partnerships with campus entrepreneurship programs across West Michigan.

Events are where most founders first meet SG. You’ll hear real founders tell real stories, meet coaches and mentors in the wild, and start to see whether this community fits who you’re becoming. For a lot of founders, one event is all it takes for them to know they’ve found their people.

**→ Find an Event**    *\[LINK TO: Events\]*

**02  ·  Connections  ·  Get Help**

Once you’ve explored, the next step is getting help. Connections pairs you 1:1 with a coach or mentor from our vetted network — experienced founders, operators, and faith-aware practitioners who’ve built what you’re building.

Coaches help you think. Mentors share what they’ve learned. Both are free to you, introduced intentionally, and matched to where you actually are — not where someone else thinks you should be. You ask for help. We make the introduction. You take it from there.

**→ Request a Connection**     *\[LINK TO: Coaches & Mentors\]*

**03  ·  Small Groups  ·  Build & Grow**

The deepest tier of the pathway. Small Groups is where founders commit to building alongside others — not just occasionally, but on a rhythm. Smaller circles. Longer relationships. Harder questions. Real accountability.

The Forge is our Small Group offering: a group of faith-driven founders who gather regularly to wrestle with the spiritual, emotional, and strategic realities of building a business that reflects who they’re becoming. Not a class. Not a program. A community.

**→ Learn About The Forge**     *\[LINK TO: The Forge\]*

**SECTION 4 — WHO WE SERVE**

**COPY NOTE:** *Inclusive framing. Warm, clear, lists kinds of founders who belong here — without being so specific it scares people off. Five asterisk bullets. Ends with a softening “and also” line so visitors know they don’t have to check every box.*

**Who Belongs Here**

Startup Garage is built for founders who want their work and their faith to belong together — not in separate compartments, but in one life. If that’s you, you’re in good company.

\*  Early-stage founders with an idea they can’t shake.

\*  Second-time founders looking for deeper community than they found the first time around.

\*  Operators, marketers, and product people dreaming about their own thing.

\*  Students and recent grads testing whether entrepreneurship is their path.

\*  Faith-driven founders who’ve felt lonely in secular rooms — and under-equipped in faith rooms.

And if you’re not sure yet — come anyway. Events are a no-risk way to find out.

**SECTION 5 — FOUNDER STORIES**

**COPY NOTE:** *Three to four full-width founder story features. Each story tells a different slice of the pathway — one about starting with events, one about a meaningful coach/mentor connection, one about the depth of The Forge (Small Groups). Use full-page features with a photo, short pull quote, 2–3 paragraph story, and the founder’s name, venture, and city. Stories may be added over time; start with placeholders.*

**Founders Walking This Pathway**

The best way to understand our pathway is to hear it from founders who’ve walked it. Different stages. Different industries. Same journey — from first event to deep community.

**Story 01  ·  From First Event to First Investor**

*“I came to one event thinking I’d grab some free pizza. I walked out with the person who would become my first investor.”*

| *\[FULL-WIDTH FEATURE — Founder Story \#1: Events → Connections\]* *Layout: Large founder photo on left, story on right (stacked on mobile).* *Content to write once a founder is selected: 2–3 short paragraphs about how the founder came to an SG event, met someone through the community, and what happened next. Emphasize the “low-stakes front door” quality of events and how connections emerged organically.* *Placeholder fields:* *• Founder Name  ·  Venture Name  ·  City* *• Pull quote (above)* *• 2–3 paragraph story* *• Optional: short “Where they are now” callout* |
| :---- |

**Story 02  ·  The Mentor Who Stayed**

*“My SG mentor has been in my corner for three years now. He’s seen the ugly. He’s still here.”*

| *\[FULL-WIDTH FEATURE — Founder Story \#2: Connections\]* *Layout: Mirror of Story 01 — photo on right, story on left. Alternating rhythm down the page.* *Content to write: 2–3 paragraphs about a long-running mentor relationship. Show how the mentorship went beyond tactics into the harder seasons — a failed raise, a co-founder conflict, a faith question the founder couldn’t ask anywhere else.* *Placeholder fields:* *• Founder Name  ·  Venture Name  ·  City* *• Mentor Name (optional)* *• Pull quote (above)* *• 2–3 paragraph story* |
| :---- |

**Story 03  ·  What The Forge Made Possible**

*“I didn’t know I was lonely until I was in a room full of people building like me.”*

| *\[FULL-WIDTH FEATURE — Founder Story \#3: Small Groups / The Forge\]* *Layout: Full-width feature with a more reflective visual treatment (maybe warmer tones or a photo from a Forge gathering).* *Content to write: 2–3 paragraphs about a founder who joined The Forge and what the depth of that community made possible — in their business, in their marriage, in their soul. This is the story that conveys why the Small Groups tier exists.* *Placeholder fields:* *• Founder Name  ·  Venture Name  ·  City* *• Pull quote (above)* *• 2–3 paragraph story* *• Optional: “What changed” short list of 2–3 before/after shifts* |
| :---- |

**Story 04  ·  Optional Fourth Feature**

**COPY NOTE:** *Room for a fourth founder feature as the story library grows. Could be a female founder, a student-founder, a second-time founder, or a founder who came in further along the pathway than Events. Julie — we can decide who fits here once the first three stories are captured.*

| *\[FULL-WIDTH FEATURE — Founder Story \#4: TBD\]* *Reserve layout space. Content to be added once a fourth founder is identified.* |
| :---- |

**SECTION 6 — WHERE TO START**

**COPY NOTE:** *Decision helper. Three short columns or stacked cards with a one-line diagnostic (“If you’re…”) and a clear next step. Self-identify, then a CTA. This is the practical close — the reader has learned about the pathway and now needs a nudge into motion.*

**Where to Start**

Not sure which step is yours? Here’s the shortcut. Pick the one that sounds most like you right now — that’s your starting point. You can always move deeper later.

**If you’re just exploring…**

You’re curious but not committed. You want to see what SG is about before you share anything about what you’re building. Come to an event. That’s all. Free, no commitment, and the best way to meet the community.

**→ Find an Event**     *\[LINK TO: Events\]*

**If you’re stuck on something…**

You’ve got an idea, a venture, or a decision, and you need help thinking it through. Request a connection. We’ll introduce you to a coach or mentor who’s been where you are.

**→ Request a Connection**     *\[LINK TO: Coaches & Mentors Intake Form\]*

**If you’re ready to go deeper…**

You’ve been around SG for a while. You’re tired of building alone. You want a rhythm of real community with other faith-driven founders. Learn about The Forge — our Small Groups community for founders who want to build together.

**→ Learn About The Forge**     *\[LINK TO: The Forge\]*

**SECTION 7 — FOOTER CTA BANNER**

**COPY NOTE:** *Match the homepage footer CTA energy. Dark background, white text, orange CTA button. Short, warm, reinforcing — the page has done the work of explaining; now we just invite.*

**Wherever You Are, There’s a Next Step.**

You don’t have to have it figured out to take the first step. You just have to take it.

**→ Find an Event**     *\[LINK TO: Events\]*

**→ Request a Connection**     *\[LINK TO: Coaches & Mentors Intake Form\]*

*Startup Garage Website Copy v1.0  |  Prepared for internal review*

# Resources & Tools

**STARTUP GARAGE**

**Website Copy — Resources & Tools**

*Version 1.0  |  For review & editing*

**COPY NOTE:** *This page is a searchable library of downloadable guides and templates for founders — organized as a filterable grid. All content is placeholder for the initial launch; the web team builds the structure and SG fills the library over time. Page also includes a callout to the deeper SG Portal (accessed by booking a meeting with the Executive Director). Voice is warm, faith-aware, practical, never corporate. Orange labels indicate copy notes or design directions for the web team.*

**SECTION 1 — HERO**

**COPY NOTE:** *Match homepage hero scale. Warm, practical opener that positions these resources as tools for the journey — not a content marketing play. One primary CTA anchors to the library grid; secondary CTA points to the SG Portal / meeting with the ED.*

**Tools for the Journey.**

Building a business is hard. A good tool at the right moment can change what’s possible — a template that saves you a week, a worksheet that reframes a stuck question, a checklist that keeps you from forgetting the thing that matters.

Everything here is free. Made for faith-driven founders. Designed to be used, not just read.

**→ Browse the Library**     *Anchor scroll to “Resource Library” section below*

**→ Access More Resources**     *Anchor scroll to “SG Portal” section below*

**SECTION 2 — HOW TO USE THIS PAGE**

**COPY NOTE:** *Short context-setter. Positions the library as practical, faith-aware, and actively curated. Four asterisk bullets describing what founders will find. Keep it crisp — this is a signpost, not a feature page.*

**What You’ll Find Here**

This is our growing library of downloadable guides and templates for early-stage founders. Practical documents you can fill in, print out, or build from. Everything is created by Startup Garage or vetted by our coaches and mentors.

\*  Business plan starters, pitch deck templates, and financial model worksheets.

\*  Operations tools — hiring checklists, founder agreements, simple contracts.

\*  Faith-at-work reflection guides and founder rhythms for the long haul.

\*  Decision frameworks for the hardest calls — co-founders, fundraising, scaling, exits.

New resources are added as coaches, mentors, and founders contribute. If something you need isn’t here yet, let us know.

**SECTION 3 — RESOURCE LIBRARY**

**COPY NOTE:** *This is the searchable/filterable grid — the core of the page. Web team builds a card grid with search and topic filters. Each card shows: title, short description, topic tags, format (PDF / Worksheet / Template), and a download button. Library starts empty and fills over time — this section is structural. See placeholder block below for card layout, filter taxonomy, and empty-state copy.*

**Browse the Library**

Filter by topic to find what you need. Click any card to download.

**Filters**

**COPY NOTE:** *Display filters as a horizontal row of pill-shaped buttons above the grid. Filters are cumulative (click multiple). Include an “All” reset and a search field on the right.*

| *\[FILTER BAR — horizontal row\]* *Topics: All  ·  Starting Up  ·  Funding  ·  Operations  ·  Marketing  ·  Leadership  ·  Faith & Work  ·  Legal & Finance* *Format: All Formats  ·  Guide  ·  Template  ·  Worksheet  ·  Checklist* *Search box (right-aligned): “Search resources…”* |
| :---- |

**Resource Cards**

**COPY NOTE:** *Grid of resource cards — 3 across on desktop, 2 on tablet, 1 on mobile. Each card is identical in structure. Library starts empty and fills over time as Julie and the SG team upload new resources. See card template below.*

| *\[RESOURCE CARD — repeated in grid\]* *Card layout (each one):* *• Small orange format tag at top  (e.g., GUIDE, TEMPLATE, WORKSHEET, CHECKLIST)* *• Resource title  (bold, \~18pt)* *• Short description  (2–3 lines max)* *• Topic tags  (gray pills, up to 3\)* *• → Download button  (orange, primary)* *On hover: subtle lift / shadow. On click: downloads the PDF or template directly — no form required for public library items.* *When library is empty (initial state):* *“The first resources land here soon. Want to be notified when new guides drop? Join the list.”  \+  email capture.* *Example card (for visual reference):* *TEMPLATE  ·  Pitch Deck Starter for Faith-Driven Founders* *A 10-slide deck built for early-stage founders who want to tell a clear story without losing what makes their venture distinct.* *Tags: Funding  ·  Starting Up  ·  Faith & Work* *→ Download* |
| :---- |

**SECTION 4 — SG PORTAL**

**COPY NOTE:** *Distinct visual treatment from the library grid — use the darker banner/accent style we use for footer CTAs. This is the “more exists beyond the public library” callout. Additional curated resources live in the SG Portal; access is unlocked through a meeting with the Executive Director. The meeting is positioned as a warm conversation, not a gate.*

**More Resources. One Conversation Away.**

Beyond the public library, Startup Garage maintains a deeper portal of curated resources — deal templates, vetted vendor lists, founder playbooks, partner introductions, and more. These are tools we’ve built or sourced for founders who are actively building.

Access starts with a conversation. Book a short meeting with our Executive Director to talk through what you’re working on and what would actually be useful. If it’s a fit, you’ll get access to the portal at the end of the call.

**Who This Is For**

\*  Founders actively building a venture who need more than starter-level tools.

\*  Faith-driven entrepreneurs looking for vetted partners and introductions.

\*  Second-time founders who’ve outgrown generic startup content.

\*  Anyone who’s been around SG and is ready for the next layer.

**→ Book a Meeting with the Executive Director**     *\[LINK TO: Scheduling Link — Julie Adkins\]*

**→ Ask a Question First**     *\[LINK TO: Contact / Email\]*

**SECTION 5 — SUGGEST A RESOURCE**

**COPY NOTE:** *Short, warm community invitation. Positions the library as a living collection the community contributes to. Light visual treatment — no big block or separate card. Just a short paragraph and a single CTA.*

**Something Missing?**

This library grows because founders, coaches, and mentors share what has actually helped them. If you have a resource that belongs here — something you built, something you love, something you wish you’d had earlier — we’d love to see it.

**→ Suggest a Resource**     *\[LINK TO: Resource Suggestion Form\]*

**SECTION 6 — FOOTER CTA BANNER**

**COPY NOTE:** *Match the homepage footer CTA energy. Dark background, white text, orange CTA button. Short, warm close. Dual CTAs so visitors can either go deeper (book the meeting) or get help now (request a connection).*

**Tools Help. People Help More.**

A great template can save you a week. A great conversation can change the direction you’re headed. Both matter. Here’s how to get both.

**→ Book a Meeting with the Executive Director**     *\[LINK TO: Scheduling Link — Julie Adkins\]*

**→ Request a Connection**     *\[LINK TO: Coaches & Mentors Intake Form\]*

*Startup Garage Website Copy v1.0  |  Prepared for internal review*
